description: A low-interest mortgage program specifically designed for lower-income first-time homebuyers to make homeownership more affordable.

income_limits.Note: Limits vary by county and family size. These are estimates based on 2024 data.

purchase_price_limits.Note: Limits vary by county and property type (1-unit vs 2-unit).

counties_served: All 62 counties in New York State
property_type_eligibility: Move-in ready single-family homes, condos, co-ops, and 2-4 unit properties.

description: A second mortgage that can be used in conjunction with any SONYMA mortgage program to help with down payment and closing costs. It has no interest and no monthly payments, and is forgiven after 10 years.
income_limits: Follows the income limits of the SONYMA mortgage program it is paired with.
purchase_price_limits: Same as the SONYMA mortgage program being used.
counties_served: All 62 counties in New York State
property_type_eligibility: Move-in ready properties eligible for SONYMA financing.

description: Provides mortgage financing to purchase and renovate a one-family home or to refinance and renovate a one-family home. This program allows homebuyers to combine the purchase price and renovation costs into a single fixed-rate mortgage.
income_limits: Follows standard SONYMA Low Interest Rate or Achieving the Dream limits depending on the borrower's eligibility.
purchase_price_limits: Follows standard SONYMA purchase price limits.
counties_served: All 62 counties in New York State
property_type_eligibility: Fixer-upper properties that need repair to meet standard living conditions.

description: Provides grants to help with your down payment and fees for getting your keys to help bridge the affordability gap.
purchase_price_limits: Varies based on specific grant funding cycles; generally follows HUD or SONYMA guidelines.
counties_served: Westchester, Nassau, Suffolk, and Dutchess counties

description: Offers financial help and counseling for first-time homebuyers, specifically focusing on residents in Ulster County.
income_limits: Generally 80% of your area's typical income (AMI) for Ulster County family sizes.
purchase_price_limits: Aligned with Ulster County typical home prices and grant requirements.
counties_served: Ulster County (primarily), with some programs extending to Sullivan and Orange counties.

program_name: City of Buffalo Down Payment Assistance
description: Provides zero-interest, forgivable loans to eligible first-time homebuyers for down payment and closing costs for homes within Buffalo city limits.
income_limits: Varies by family size; generally 80% AMI for Erie County.
purchase_price_limits: Must meet HUD's FHA mortgage limits for Erie County.
counties_served: Erie (City of Buffalo only)

program_name: City of Rochester Homebuyer Assistance Program
description: Provides grants to low-and-moderate income first-time homebuyers to assist with down payment and closing costs for properties in Rochester.
income_limits: Up to 80% AMI for Monroe County.
purchase_price_limits: Subject to HUD's HOME Program price limits.
counties_served: Monroe (City of Rochester only)
property_type_eligibility: Single-family homes, townhouses, and condos in the city.

program_name: City of Albany Down Payment Assistance
description: Assistance for first-time homebuyers purchasing a home in the City of Albany. The program provides a grant to help with down payment and fees for getting your keys.
income_limits: Based on 80% of Area Median Income for the Albany-Schenectady-Troy MSA.
purchase_price_limits: Must not exceed HUD 203(b) mortgage limits for Albany County.
counties_served: Albany (City of Albany only)

description: Offers fixed-rate mortgages with interest rates 0.375% lower than the standard SONYMA interest rates and no points or origination fees. Available to military veterans, active duty service members, and their spouses.
income_limits: Follows standard SONYMA Low Interest Rate limits. Note: Military pay for active duty service members serving in a combat zone may be excluded.
purchase_price_limits: Follows standard SONYMA purchase price limits.
counties_served: All 62 counties in New York State

description: Provides no-cost energy efficiency improvements to low- and moderate-income households. Improvements may include insulation, air sealing, energy-efficient lighting, and health and safety measures.

income_limits.Basis: 60% of State Median Income (SMI) for low-income; up to 80% AMI for moderate-income.

counties_served: All 62 counties in New York State
purchase_price_limits: Varies by county and property type.

description: Provides financial assistance to make residential units accessible for low- and moderate-income persons with disabilities. Grants are used for modifications such as ramps, lifts, and bathroom adaptations.
income_limits: Up to 80% of Area Median Income (AMI).
counties_served: All 62 counties in New York State (via local nonprofit administrators)
purchase_price_limits: Varies by county and property type.

description: HUD's primary program for the rehabilitation and repair of single-family properties. Allows homebuyers to finance both the purchase and the repair of a home with a single mortgage loan.
income_limits: No income limits. Eligibility is based on standard FHA credit and debt-to-income requirements.
purchase_price_limits: Limited by FHA county loan limits (varies by county, approx. $498,000 to $1,150,000 in NY).
counties_served: All 62 counties in New York State

description: Comprehensive consumer portal providing resources, educational tools, and assistance options for homebuyers, homeowners, and renters. Covers mortgage products, foreclosure prevention, and rental assistance.
income_limits: Varies by specific assistance program; general resources have no income limits.
counties_served: All 62 counties in New York State
purchase_price_limits: Varies by specific product.
property_type_eligibility: Includes single-family homes, condos, co-ops, and rental properties.

description: Provides payment assistance to low- and very-low-income applicants in eligible rural areas. The assistance reduces the mortgage payment to an effective interest rate as low as 1%.
income_limits: Varies by county; typically 50% AMI (Very Low) to 80% AMI (Low).
counties_served: Eligible rural areas in New York State (all counties except highly urbanized areas of NYC, Buffalo, etc.)
purchase_price_limits: Varies by county and property type.

description: Provides loans to very-low-income homeowners to repair, improve or modernize their homes or grants to elderly very-low-income homeowners to remove health and safety hazards.
income_limits: Household income must be below 50% of the Area Median Income (Very Low).
counties_served: Eligible rural areas in New York State
purchase_price_limits: Varies by county and property type.

description: Offers incentives for homebuyers purchasing homes in designated communities, including up to $20,000 for renovations. Provides lower interest rates and down payment assistance. This program is particularly beneficial for 'Next Step' borrowers and those in special occupations such as teachers, police officers, firefighters, and EMS personnel.
income_limits: Follows standard SONYMA limits (Low Interest Rate or ATD).
counties_served: Designated communities in Albany, Clinton, Dutchess, Erie, Monroe, Montgomery, Nassau, Niagara, Oneida, Onondaga, Orange, Rensselaer, Schenectady, Suffolk, and Westchester.
purchase_price_limits: Varies by county and property type.

description: Federally funded programs (HOME Investment Partnerships and Community Development Block Grants) administered by local counties and municipalities. Typically provides $5,000 to $20,000 in deferred-payment loans for down payment and closing costs.
income_limits: Usually capped at 80% of Area Median Income (AMI).
official_url: Contact local county housing office (e.g., Erie County, Monroe County, Westchester County).
counties_served: Many counties and larger 'Entitlement' cities across New York State.
purchase_price_limits: Varies by county and property type.

description: Nonprofit-administered loans up to $5,000 to help first-time homebuyers cover closing costs, at a simple interest rate of 3-6% depending on income, payable over 5 years.
income_limits: No hard cap; buyers below 80% of HUD area median income qualify for the lower 3% interest rate.
purchase_price_limits: Not specified; standard first mortgage qualification applies.
counties_served: All of New York State except New York City
property_type_eligibility: Owner-occupied primary residence; first-time homebuyers only.

program_name: Habitat for Humanity of New York State
description: A statewide network of 30 local Habitat affiliates helping income-eligible families become homeowners through affordable, sweat-equity-built or renovated homes. Not a cash grant — buyers invest hundreds of hours of their own labor alongside volunteers, and homes are sold to them at an affordable rate with an affordable mortgage.
income_limits: Varies by local affiliate; generally targets low-to-moderate income households below area median income.
purchase_price_limits: Not applicable — Habitat builds or renovates the home itself at an affordable price point.
counties_served: All 62 counties in New York State
property_type_eligibility: Single-family homes built or renovated by the local Habitat affiliate.

description: Westchester County's own down payment and closing cost assistance program, providing up to $25,000 to income-eligible first-time homebuyers purchasing a house, condominium, or co-op in the county.
income_limits: Income-eligible first-time homebuyers; specific limits set annually by Westchester County.
purchase_price_limits: Varies by purchase type (house, condo, co-op); confirm current limits with the county.
counties_served: Westchester

program_name: City of New Rochelle Down Payment Assistance Program (DPAP)
description: A forgivable, zero-interest second mortgage covering up to 19% of a home's purchase price for eligible first-time homebuyers. Fully forgiven after 5 years of owner-occupancy. As of May 2026, eligibility expanded beyond current residents to also include people who work full-time in New Rochelle, business owners based in the city, and former residents who lived there at least 2 years.
income_limits: Household income at or below 80% of Area Median Income (AMI) for Westchester County.
purchase_price_limits: Purchase price limits apply per HUD guidelines; confirm current limit with Westchester Residential Opportunities (WRO), the program administrator.
counties_served: Westchester (City of New Rochelle only)
property_type_eligibility: Owner-occupied single-family homes within New Rochelle city limits.
